rationale: Keep: the cited 2003 JMRK paper is directly about correction methods for outcome-dependent/selective sampling in rare-event binary choice (including churn), while the citing 2006 paper explicitly studies balanced rare-event sampling and compares weighting vs intercept correction across logit, bagging, and boosting. The citing note says it extends prior logit-focused correction practice, making this cited work a plausible methodological/foundational source rather than an incidental mention.
